name: Credit Union One Of Oklahoma
description_short: Credit Union One of Oklahoma is a federally-insured credit union based in Oklahoma City, OK. Member-owned and not-for-profit, it provides everyday banking, lending, and savings services to its membership.
description_long: Credit Union One Of Oklahoma is a state-chartered credit union serving members in , , established in 1949. As a not-for-profit financial cooperative, credit unions typically offer lower loan rates, higher savings rates, and fewer fees than traditional banks. The credit union operates 3 branch locations. Deposits are federally insured up to $250,000 by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA).
services: Personal loans (typically lower rates than banks)
pros: Not-for-profit — typically lower fees and better rates than banks
NCUA insured — deposits protected up to $250,000
cons: Membership requirements may apply (geographic, employer, or affiliation)
Fewer branches and ATMs than large banks
May have limited digital banking features
best_for: Lower fees and better loan rates than banks
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Credit Union One of Oklahoma (Oklahoma City, OK) | CreditDoc
meta_description: Credit Union One of Oklahoma — NCUA-insured credit union in Oklahoma City, OK. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
